raw_consumer_statement:
I ordered a dress from XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX  for {$610.00}. The dress was delivered at XXXX XXXX  on XX/XX/year>. I opened the box, discovered that the dress has dar red stains on the neck, contacted customer service at XXXX XXXX  the same day, took pictures of the stains, and returned the dress following their instructions. They did not respond or issue a refund, so I filed a dispute with Citibank credit card dispute dept. I received a message from XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX about a shipment from XXXX -- they returned the dress 3 months later because " it was unusable ''. I still have the dress and I can submit photos -- it looks the same : XXXX XXXX  on the back, the way I received it. They note their return policy that the items have to be " unused '' in order to be returned and list the same pictures as the reason for not accepting the return. 
I submitted all the documentation to citi : communication with XXXX, XXXX delivery time, and photos. I ask CFPB to review this case because I have timely and thorough documentation of receipt and return of the item. Most purchases have at least a 7 day return policy while I reached out to the merchant within XXXX minutes with the issue. How can I prove to them that I did not stain the dress?
